data.title: Binance CEO Hails SEC Lawsuit Dismissal as ‘Significant Win,’ Credits Trump for Crypto Shift
data.description:
Binance CEO Richard Teng has welcomed the SEC’s motion to dismiss its 2023 securities complaint, framing the move as a win that eases uncertainty for the exchange and wider market. Teng said new policy direction under President Trump and a Crypto Task Force could help the US become a leading hub.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/binance-ceo-hails-sec-lawsuit-dismissal-as-significant-win-credits-trump-for-crypto-shift/

data.description:

data.title: Warren Targets GOP’s Crypto Wallet Loophole in GENIUS Act – Will Her Amendment Block Trump’s Wallet Ambitions?
data.description:
Elizabeth Warren has moved to tighten the GENIUS Act, proposing an amendment barring sitting or former presidents and their families from profiting via stablecoins or wallet platforms. She says a late exemption could let Donald Trump’s USD1 venture avoid federal oversight.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/genius-act-crypto-wallet-loophole/

data.title: BlackRock Bitcoin Futures ETF Launches on Moscow Exchange Amid Privacy Concerns
data.description:
The Moscow Exchange has added BlackRock’s IBIT-9. 25 Bitcoin Futures ETF, giving qualified investors access while full compliance checks start on June 23. Yet Rosfinmonitoring has noted that crypto exchanges share wallet data with police, challenging privacy hopes in Russia’s developing crypto market
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/blackrock-bitcoin-futures-etf-launch-moscow-exchange/

data.title: JPMorgan Chase to accept cryptocurrency ETFs as loan collateral
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News JPMorgan Chase is preparing to accept cryptocurrency-related assets as collateral for loans, including Bitcoin exchange-traded funds. The bank will first provide financing services for cryptocurrency ETFs, with the first batch involving BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust. The move marks a major expansion of JPMorgan Chase's cryptocurrency-related business, and comes at a time when the overall regulatory environment is loosening under the new US government. 
Previously, JPMorgan Chase accepted such collateral only on a limited case-by-case basis. But under the new plan, cryptocurrency ETFs will be treated more like traditional assets (such as stocks or real estate) when assessing customers' borrowing capacity. The report also pointed out that the plan, which is expected to be launched in the coming weeks, may include more cryptocurrency investment products. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin Breakthrough: JPMorgan to Offer Crypto Services Despite Jamie Dimon’s Past Criticism
data.description:
(NYSE: JPM), a top-tier financial institution with a sprawling global presence, is expanding its crypto offerings led by Bitcoin (BTC). According to a report by Bloomberg, JPMorgan, which manages more than $4. 3 trillion in customers’ assets, plans to consider Bitcoin and other digital assets when assessing clients’ overall net worth
source: https://coinpedia.org/news/bitcoin-breakthrough-jpmorgan-to-offer-crypto-services-despite-jamie-dimons-past-criticism/
